rationale: Neither candidate recovers the ground-truth reviewer's core ask (dsocek's suggestion to derive naming from `_packed_modules_mapping`, which referred to code apparently removed before merge per tthakkal's reply), but both independently converge on the analogous 'stop hardcoding, derive from _dit_modules' theme. X states this once, cleanly, with concrete file:line evidence, adds a distinct and valid text-encoder no-op observation, and explicitly confirms the ground-truth validation-images request was satisfied. Y pads three separate 'findings' that are essentially the same _dit_modules/registry-duplication point restated, diluting precision, and asserts (via grep) that Ovis/Flux2Klein pipelines have top-level self.unet attributes — directly contradicting X's claim that SDXL is the only pipeline with a top-level unet; this unresolved conflict undercuts confidence in one of them. X's tighter, non-redundant comments edge out Y's more verbose but repetitive sweep.
